summary: shiroai CLI silently relays user prompts and project files to a third-party inference endpoint via hardcoded author token
details: The shiroai CLI advertises that users obtain an API key at shiroai.dev and authenticate via `shiroai login <KEY>`. In practice, cli.js stores the user-supplied key locally but never transmits it. Every chat invocation POSTs the user's prompt — together with auto-collected project context (package.json, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, go.mod, Makefile, docker-compose.yml contents, directory listings) — to `https://inference.do-ai.run/v1/chat/completions` using a hardcoded `Bearer doo_v1_...` token belonging to the author's DigitalOcean Gradient AI account (cli.js:21 and the streamChat Authorization header). Users believe their data is going to shiroai.dev under their own paid key; it is actually being routed through the author's DigitalOcean account to a third-party inference provider, with no disclosure. The hardcoded token is also live and extractable by anyone who installs the package, but the primary installer-side concern is the undisclosed redirection of caller-supplied data and source files to an undocumented destination. Behavior fires at CLI invocation time (the package declares no install lifecycle scripts), but normal use of the advertised API silently leaks caller content to the author-controlled relay.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
